behaviors.description: Validate required patient fields before downstream steps; return one error listing all missing fields.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is validator-to-orchestrator handoff: behavior requires both correct missing-field detection and that downstream processing does not run. Rejected System because behavior text is only field validation/gating, with no required real OS/network/subprocess. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no AI decision is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Run intake orchestrator with inputs missing multiple required fields and in-process stubs/spies for insurance/triage/scheduling; assert error lists all missing fields and no downstream module is invoked.

behaviors.description: DOB validation rejects future dates, dates older than 150 years, and non-date strings.
behaviors.justification: Single validation logic boundary, no module interaction needed to expose the failure.
behaviors.test_description: Directly test DOB validator with representative valid/invalid inputs (future, >150 years old, malformed string) and assert validation errors.

behaviors.description: Insurance check sends correct patient ID/plan code and maps payer statuses eligible/not eligible/pending.
behaviors.justification: Needs interaction between insurance service, request builder, and response mapper. Rejected System: behavior does not require real payer network behavior, only payload correctness and status interpretation. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no AI output dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Wire insurance service to a stub HTTP payer endpoint/client fixture; assert outbound payload contains expected patient ID/plan code and each status response maps to internal eligibility state correctly.

behaviors.description: If payer API is unreachable, mark eligibility pending and continue intake.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at module handoff (payer client error handling into pipeline continuation). Rejected System because unreachable can be reproduced by deterministic timeout/connection-error stubs. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no AI-quality dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Execute intake flow with payer client configured to throw timeout/connection errors; assert eligibility becomes pending and subsequent triage/scheduling/confirmation steps still execute.

behaviors.description: Triage call payload includes reported symptoms and configured emergency rubric.
behaviors.justification: This tests LLM-call plumbing/payload assembly, not model judgment quality. Rejected Agentic based on behavior text focusing on input payload contents, which can be validated with a stubbed model endpoint.
behaviors.test_description: Run triage invocation through service layer with stubbed model client; assert outbound request includes exact symptoms and emergency rubric fields.

behaviors.description: Live AI triage assigns high urgency to emergency symptom combinations (e.g., chest pain + shortness of breath).
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model judgment over clinical symptom combinations; stubs would hide true misclassification risk.
behaviors.test_description: Send real triage requests for emergency symptom scenarios to the live AI engine across repeated runs and assert high-urgency classification rate meets threshold.

behaviors.description: Triage result parser stores clinician-readable explanation field, not only numeric score.
behaviors.justification: Needs parser-to-persistence interaction verification (field survives mapping/storage). Rejected Unit because storage handoff is part of the behavior. Rejected Agentic because behavior checks field presence/propagation, not explanation quality.
behaviors.test_description: Feed deterministic triage responses containing score+explanation through parser and repository; assert persisted record includes explanation text column/value.

behaviors.description: Live AI triage explanation is case-specific and includes symptom-based rationale, not boilerplate.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly evaluates generation quality/relevance of model output; mocks cannot detect generic-but-plausible explanations.
behaviors.test_description: Run live triage on varied symptom inputs and evaluate explanations for symptom mentions and rationale specificity using rubric/annotated checks.

behaviors.description: Scheduler picks first available slot consistent with urgency SLA (high same-day, medium within 48h).
behaviors.justification: Requires scheduling policy + calendar data interaction. Rejected System: can be validated with local/in-process calendar DB fixture. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no model output dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Populate test calendar with controlled availability, invoke scheduling for high/medium urgencies, and assert earliest slot selection satisfies same-day/48-hour constraints.

behaviors.description: Concurrent patient processing does not double-book the same time slot.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary is concurrent access and transactional integrity across scheduling modules and shared calendar DB. Rejected System: no external OS/network/subprocess requirement beyond local DB concurrency semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Run two concurrent scheduling requests against the shared test calendar DB for one remaining slot; assert one succeeds and the other is rejected/retried without duplicate booking.

behaviors.description: Confirmation message includes required fields and visit-type-appropriate preparation instructions.
behaviors.justification: Often fails at data composition/template handoff between appointment context and messaging module. Rejected System: no real external transport needed to verify content. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: deterministic templating behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Build confirmation through application layer from scheduled appointment + visit type fixtures; assert rendered message contains patient name, time, provider, and correct prep instructions.

behaviors.description: Orchestration pipeline completes correctly with deterministic stubbed triage output.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly says deterministic stubbed triage, so this is module orchestration/state handoff, not model-quality validation. Rejected Agentic via disqualifying text: "deterministic stubbed triage output." Rejected System unless real infra is introduced by test design.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end intake in-process with stubbed triage (and controllable insurance/scheduler fakes), asserting stage order, state propagation, and successful completion.

behaviors.description: Full live intake pipeline completes for a real patient submission without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: Pipeline includes a real AI triage call; execution path depends on live model behavior and cannot be fully validated with stubs. Rejected Workflow because only one agentic decision stage is described (triage), not multiple agentic steps in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run production-like intake against live dependencies (real payer API and live triage engine) for a controlled real submission and assert automated completion through confirmation.
